title: Oreogrammitis nigropaleata
taxonomy: Oreogrammitis nigropaleata is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. It belongs to the genus Oreogrammitis, which is a group of epiphytic ferns native to Australia and New Guinea. The classification of this species is within the family Polypodiaceae, which is a part of the larger group Polypodiales, an order of ferns. Oreogrammitis nigropaleata is also classified as a member of the class Polypodiopsida, which includes all the ferns. This species' classification helps in understanding its evolutionary relationships and characteristics.
uses: Oreogrammitis nigropaleata, a species of fern found in the tropical forests of Southeast Asia, has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits. The plant's leaves are said to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are believed to aid in the treatment of various ailments. In traditional remedies, the leaves of Oreogrammitis nigropaleata are often used to treat fever, cough, and sore throats. Further research is needed to confirm the effectiveness of the plant's medicinal properties. However, its use in traditional medicine highlights the potential of this fern in providing new leads for modern medicine.

preparations: Oreogrammitis nigropaleata is a species of fern, but there is limited information available about its traditional or herbal preparations.
